closed_children.title: display.rs module: color detection, glyphs, tree primitives
bl dep tree: actual tree rendering with box-drawing
bl list redesign: status column, grouping, nesting under parents
bl ready redesign: status column + parent hint line
bl show redesign: header, relations block, notes, wrapping

description:
Today bl list / bl ready / bl show / bl dep tree are plain-text, uncolored, and bl dep tree is misnamed — it's a flat list, not a tree. Beads feels more polished in comparison. This epic is the overhaul.

Design anchors:
- Glyphs are scannable anchors; status words always render alongside them (not a replacement). Users shouldn't have to memorize icons.
- Colors are additive, never load-bearing. Respect NO_COLOR, CLICOLOR=0, and non-TTY stdout. --plain forces unstyled regardless of terminal.
- Machine contract unchanged: --json output is byte-identical to today.

Visual spec:

Status glyphs (always paired with words; Unicode default, ASCII fallback on non-TTY/NO_COLOR/--plain):
  open         ○  dim       ([ ])
  in_progress  »  yellow    ([>])
  review       ?  cyan      ([?])
  blocked      ⌀  magenta   ([!])
  closed       ✓  green     ([x])
  deferred     ~  dim gray  ([-])

Priority dot replaces [P3] prefix: ● red/yellow/blue/dim for 1/2/3/4.

ID badges (after the id, before the title):
  ★ claimed by you (matches BALLS_IDENTITY)
  ◆ has unmet deps
  ⛓ has open gates links

Column layout for list/ready:
  ● » in_progress   bl-5678 ★ Swap middleware                    api, auth

Library: owo-colors (pure, no deps) or anstream (Windows-aware). Pick one in the first child ball.

Dependencies:
- bl-91e0 (display module) is prerequisite for all the others.
- The consumer balls (list, ready, show, dep tree, progress bars) can go in any order once the module lands.

Forward-compat:
- Output format is human-facing; no on-disk changes. Nothing to round-trip.
- --json paths are not touched.
title: CLI display overhaul: colors, glyphs, real trees, polished output
